articles.title: Dollar Rallies on Crude Oil Strength as Hot US CPI Report
articles.expand: The dollar index (DXY00 ) today is up by +0.45%. The dollar is climbing today on renewed concerns that the US-Iran ceasefire may break down after President Trump said the current ceasefire was on "life support." Today's +3% surge in crude oil prices also boosts inflation expectations and may prompt...

Don't think you need Xi's help on Iran The blockade has been 100% successful You are going to have s gusher of oil If you look back 3-4 months ago, you would think oil would be much higher Iran will not have a nuclear weapon. articles.title: The GBPUSD is the biggest mover today. Sellers taking back some control
articles.expand: While UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer hangs on politically, the GBPUSD is moving sharply lower and is currently the biggest decliner among the major currency pairs against the stronger US dollar. The pair is down around -0.65% on the day, with sellers maintaining control after key technical breaks during the Asian-Pacific and European sessions. Tech...

articles.title: Oil: Iran tensions and China demand shifts – Commerzbank
articles.expand: Commerzbank’s Barbara Lambrecht and colleagues note Brent has jumped as Iran–US tensions escalate and US policy debates gasoline tax relief. China’s crude Oil imports fell sharply in April, with further declines expected as shipments from Saudi Arabia and Iran drop.

articles.title: WTI Price Forecast: Oil prices remain underpinned by Hormuz tensions and tighter supply outlook
articles.expand: West Texas Intermediate (WTI) crude Oil rallies more than 3% on Tuesday as fading hopes for a near-term end to the US-Iran war continue to fuel concerns over supply disruptions through the Strait of Hormuz.

articles.title: Fed's Goolsbee:Inflation is going the wrong way, not just in oil and tariff related things
articles.expand: More from Fed's Goolsbee: inflation is going the wrong way, not just in oil and tariff related things. Drift upward and services inflation is a worry. Today's unexpected disappointment was seeing services inflation going up Labor market is stable but it is not good The job market is basically stable but inflation is going up. Remains optimistic tha...

articles.title: US Dollar: Neutral range trading outlook – TD Securities
articles.expand: TD Securities’ FX team, led by Jayati Bharadwaj and colleagues, keeps a neutral short-term stance on the US Dollar (USD) following the April United States (US) Consumer Price Index (CPI) release.

articles.title: New Zealand Dollar declines amid strong US inflation as focus shifts to RBNZ outlook
articles.expand: NZD/USD trades around 0.5940 on Tuesday at the time of writing, down 0.41% on the day, pressured by stronger-than-expected inflation data from the United States (US).

articles.title: Warsh for Fed Board squeaks through Senate 51-45, Fed Chair vote on deck for Wednesday
articles.expand: Kevin Warsh squeaked through the Senate 51-45 on Tuesday to claim a seat on the Federal Reserve (Fed) Board of Governors, with the separate vote to confirm him as Fed Chair scheduled for Wednesday and Jerome Powell's term as chair wrapping up on Friday.

articles.title: Kevin Warsh is confirmed to be a Federal Governor of the Federal Reserve Board
articles.expand: The Senate has confirmed Kevin Warsh as a Federal Reserve Governor on the Federal Reserve Board. The next step is to nominate and vote on him as the next Fed chair ahead of the May 15 deadline. The vote is to take place either tomorrow or on Thursday. Current Fed chair Powell, will then be replaced but Powell’s term as a Board member runs until Jan...

articles.title: Pound Sterling Price News and Forecast:Falls as US inflation surprises, UK political turmoil weighs
articles.expand: GBP/USD declines around 1.3525 on Tuesday at the time of writing, down 0.62% on the day, as the US Dollar (USD) benefits from renewed demand following stronger-than-expected US inflation data and a more cautious market mood. Read More...

articles.title: Australian Dollar drops as hot US CPI strengthens Fed higher-for-longer outlook
articles.expand: The AUD/USD pair fell toward the 0.7220 region on Tuesday after hotter-than-expected United States (US) inflation data boosted the US Dollar (USD) and reinforced expectations that the Federal Reserve (Fed) may keep interest rates elevated for longer.

articles.title: Gold drops below $4,700 as strong US CPI lifts US Dollar and yields
articles.expand: Gold (XAU/USD) extends its slide on Tuesday, retracing the previous day’s gains as hotter-than-expected US inflation data boosts US Treasury yields and the US Dollar (USD).

articles.title: Japanese Yen dips as US inflation surprise lifts Dollar, hawkish BoJ anchors JPY
articles.expand: USD/JPY trades around 157.65 on Tuesday at the time of writing, up 0.30% on the day.

articles.title: Canadian Dollar declines after strong US inflation data reinforces hawkish Fed outlook
articles.expand: USD/CAD trades with a positive tone on Tuesday as a stronger US Dollar (USD) offsets support from elevated Oil prices for the Canadian Dollar (CAD). At the time of writing, the pair is trading around 1.3715, hovering near its highest level since April 16.

articles.title: EURUSD breaks below 200 hour MA. Short term bias shift
articles.expand: The EURUSD is breaking below its 200-hour moving average at 1.17367 (green line on the chart below), a move that tilts the short-term bias more to the downside. Earlier today, the pair also moved below the 100-hour moving average, currently at 1.17577 (blue line), signaling an initial shift away from the bullish bias that had supported the pair in ...

articles.title: The US Dollar rallies back after CPI, is the correction is over ? – EUR/USD, GBP/USD & Dollar Index (DXY) overview
articles.expand: Forex Market update: The US Dollar rallies sharply as domestic inflation beats expectations and geopolitical uncertainty persists. As EUR/USD and GBP/USD reverse their recent gains amid triple-digit oil prices and fading peace hopes, explore our technical analysis to determine if the Dollar's correction is officially over.

articles.title: Fed: AI-driven growth complicates rate path – NBC
articles.expand: National Bank of Canada’s (NBC) Senior Economist Jocelyn Paquet, argues that surging AI-related investment is keeping U.S. GDP growth above potential, with forecasts of 2.4% in 2026 and 2.0% in 2027.

articles.title: Pound Sterling Price News and Forecast: GBP/USD slides toward trendline support below 1.3600
articles.expand: GBP/USD is losing ground below the 1.3600 handle, eyeing support at the medium‑term ascending trendline. The pound is under pressure against the dollar amid domestic political uncertainty, lingering Middle East tensions, and ahead of key US CPI data.

articles.title: Japanese Yen: Intervention risk stays elevated – MUFG
articles.expand: MUFG’s Lee Hardman notes that the US Dollar’s (USD) rebound alongside higher Oil prices has lifted USD/JPY back towards 158.00, close to levels seen before suspected Japanese intervention.

articles.expand: Our mandate requires us to act if inflation expectations de-anchor Data will determine ECB's decision in June Baseline included two rate hikes The ECB has already signalled that a rate hike in June is coming unless the war ends and oil prices fall significantly before then, so I'm not sure why he places weight on the data. articles.title: NZDJPY Flashback Update: Preparing For Another Wave Higher
articles.expand: NZDJPY remains below the 95 resistance zone and could still be forming a wave four correction before another push higher toward 96–97.

articles.title: Indian central bank likely selling dollars to limit Indian rupee's slide, traders say
articles.expand: MUMBAI: The Reserve Bank of India likely intervened in the foreign exchange market to support the rupee on Tuesday, three traders told Reuters , as worries over the fraying ceasefire between U.S. and Iran lifted oil prices, hurting the South Asian currency. The Indian rupee fell to a record low of 95.6250 per dollar before paring losses to last quo...

articles.title: Bank Indonesia May Lift Benchmark Rate to 5% as Rupiah Weakens
articles.expand: Bank Indonesia is predicted to raise the benchmark interest rate in May or June 2026 if the rupiah continues to weaken.

articles.title: Warsh's red lines worry world finance
articles.expand: Kevin Warsh is poised to take the helm of the Federal Reserve with a mission to clip the wings of a central bank he thinks strayed too far into politics. Many countries fear that just gives U.S. politicians more room to use financial tools as a cudgel.

articles.title: 'Bitcoin transactions can be monitored’: Ray Dalio explains why central banks won’t touch BTC
articles.expand: Ray Dalio joins the privacy debate, saying Bitcoin’s full transparency makes it less likely to be adopted by central banks.

articles.title: Iran war oil shortage forces Japan snack giant to use black-and-white packaging

articles.title: Global-AI rally pauses as Middle East ceasefire goes on ‘life support’
articles.expand: New Delhi: Oil crept higher and the dollar rose on Tuesday as hopes faded for a deal to get ships moving through the Strait of Hormuz, while a red-hot chip rally in chip stocks cooled and traders waited on U.S. inflation figures. U.S. President Donald Trump said the ceasefire with Iran was “on life support” [...]

articles.title: Dollar steady as Middle East peace hopes recede
articles.expand: SINGAPORE: The U.S. dollar held its ground on Tuesday as talks to end the war in the Middle East showed no signs of progress, pushing oil prices higher and worrying investors that interest rates may need to stay higher to tackle inflationary pressures. Investors now fear that the ceasefire that has been in place since April 7 could be in danger and...

articles.title: Critical Resources expands lithium drill pipeline in Canada
articles.expand: Critical Resources has launched fieldwork at its Corona target in Canada, chasing concealed lithium pegmatites across a 5km corridor north of its 8Mt Mavis Lake resource.

articles.title: FX Watch: EUR/USD and USD/JPY Setups to Watch if U.S. CPI Comes in Hot
articles.expand: If Uncle Sam drops a much hotter inflation picture, USD traders may want to check EUR/USD and USD/JPY charts for opportunities.

articles.title: Alphabet plans first yen bond sale to fund $190 billion AI spending push
articles.expand: Alphabet is planning its first yen-denominated bond sale across up to eight maturities, expected to total several hundred billion yen, as part of a broader push to fund $190 billion in AI capital spending this year. articles.expand: Japan and the US reaffirmed close coordination on currency markets, including intervention, after Finance Minister Katayama met Bessent in Tokyo, as Japan is suspected to have spent $63.5 billion defending the yen. articles.expand: Bank of Japan’s Summary of Opinions from the April meeting revealed a noticeably more hawkish debate inside the board, with several policymakers arguing that another interest rate hike could come sooner rather than later as the Iran war intensifies inflation pressures. articles.title: FX Watch: USD/CHF and AUD/USD Triangle Breakouts If U.S. CPI Fails to Impress
articles.expand: Markets are expecting a significantly upbeat U.S. CPI to tip the scales in favor of Fed tightening soon, but what if the numbers fail to impress? Here’s what I’m watching on USD/CHF and AUD/USD in this scenario.

articles.title: Australian business confidence remains deeply pessimistic, -24 in April (prior -29)
articles.expand: Australian business confidence edged up to -24 in April from -29 in March but remained deeply negative, as surging energy costs squeezed margins and conditions fell to their lowest since 2020, NAB data showed. articles.title: BOJ 'Summary" - Japan rate hike back on table as BOJ signals next move still likely upward
articles.expand: The Bank of Japan held rates at its April meeting but warned upside inflation risks are rising as crude oil prices surge, with several members flagging possible hikes as soon as the next meeting. Summary: The Bank of Japan kept its policy interest rate unchanged at its April 27-28 meeting, citing the need for a wait-and-see approach given the uncer...

articles.title: UK consumer spending falls for first time since 2024 as Iran war bites
articles.expand: UK consumer spending fell 0.1% in April, the first annual decline since late 2024, as Iran war fears drove households toward savings and away from big-ticket and travel spending, Barclays and BRC data showed. Summary: Barclays credit and debit card spending fell 0.1% year-on-year in April, reversing a 0.9% gain in March, the first decline since Nov...

articles.title: Economic and event calendar Asia Tuesday, May 12, 2026 - BoJ will give us their thoughts
articles.expand: The Bank of Japan will release its 'Summary' of the April meeting today. This was an 'on hold' meeting, but was eventful. articles.title: Australian Dollar Price Forecast: Extra gains still on the cards
articles.expand: The Aussie Dollar continues its march north almost unabated, lifting AUD/USD to the area of multi-year tops. For now, the pair’s positive outlook remains unchanged, propped up by elevated inflation in Oz and the RBA’s hawkish stance.

articles.expand: The USDCHF continues to lean against its 100-hour moving average as a key resistance barometer. Corrective rallies last week stalled against that moving average on Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday, and Friday — and today’s rally attempt once again failed near the same level. articles.title: Weekly Forex Forecast – AUD/USD, S&P 500 Index, NASDAQ 100 Index, Brent Crude Oil, Gasoline
articles.expand: As the prospects of a peace deal between the USA and Iran seemed to rise again, risk assets and stocks rose last week, while energy prices took a hit. Find out if a turnaround might happen this week as we face another Trump war deadline due Thursday.
